trigger.reason: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 with stronger agentic capabilities and significantly lower pricing, directly competing with Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro—a model release with explicit cost-reduction positioning that will trigger broad sympathy-fade selling in agent-software and enterprise-SaaS adjacencies.

primary_event.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
primary_event.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, positioned as a more capable and significantly cheaper alternative to its Opus tier, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro for agentic workloads. The model delivers stronger agentic capabilities at lower price points, directly competing on cost in a market where enterprises evaluate AI tooling ROI.

secondary_events.headline: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
Anthropic's Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Anthropic's launch of Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper agentic model, with secondary blast radius into agent-software and enterprise-SaaS adjacencies.
discovery.run_summary: The candidate set is dominated by green-tape sympathy moves UP (semis, cyber, machine vision) rather than panic selloffs, suggesting today's tape is mostly an AI-beneficiary rally rather than an AI-sympathy fade. Only two candidates (EVH, VRRM) moved meaningfully down, and neither has a clean overlap with what Sonnet 5 actually substitutes for. Most curation hypotheses are confirmed by filings (AI is mentioned as a tailwind or peripheral, not a core substitution threat), but with negligible negative price action there are few clean OVERDONE setups today.

discoveries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors describe revenue as flowing from payer contracts, specialty network risk-sharing, clinical risk arrangements with Centene/Molina/Florida Blue/Cook County, and CMS-regulated value-based care. AI is mentioned only as a general technology risk (use of AI/ML models), not as a substitution threat to the contract-and-network business model.
discoveries.thesis: Evolent's product is regulated payer contracts plus specialty clinical networks plus risk-sharing capital — a cheaper Anthropic agent model does not displace a Medicaid managed-care contract or an oncology risk corridor. The down move on an Anthropic agentic-model day is thematic SaaS-sympathy selling against a name whose real risks (Medicaid redeterminations, oncology cost trend, OBBBA policy changes) have nothing to do with LLM pricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: EVH bounces in the next 1-3 sessions while other healthcare-IT names that actually do compete with workflow AI underperform; no new payer contract or oncology-cost disclosure follows.
discoveries.what_kills: A subsequent customer-loss 8-K or downward guide tied to a specific payer renegotiation — those would reveal the move was idiosyncratic, not sympathy.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Healthcare value-based care names get lumped into 'SaaS' baskets on AI-lab news days even though the product is a regulated contract, not software — a recurring false-victim pattern worth tracking.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: EVH was flagged because cheaper agentic AI is narratively positioned as a threat to value-based-care management software, placing it in the enterprise-SaaS blast radius the trigger identified.
research_pointers: Check whether the down move correlates with broader healthcare-services index move or is name-specific
Confirm no concurrent 8-K (payer loss, guidance cut, going-concern language) on 6/30
Track oncology cost trend commentary on next earnings call — the real risk vector here

Down on the day but the curation hypothesis (physical cameras + municipal contracts) is so far removed from Sonnet 5's agentic-software substitution that this looks more like idiosyncratic weakness (Ontario speed-camera ban disclosed in 10-K, NYCDOT contract renegotiation with tougher terms) than a clean AI sympathy fade; the filings actively flag real headwinds, so panic_calibration is closer to 'partial' than 'unjustified' — not a clean OVERDONE setup.

discovery.no_signals_note: Today's tape shows AI-beneficiary names being bid, not faded — the sympathy-fade screen has limited raw material. Only EVH presents a defensible OVERDONE read, and even that is medium-confidence given a small absolute move and a curated thesis (contract-and-network moat) that the filings confirm but the market may not be specifically punishing today.

_trigger.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
_trigger.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, positioned as a more capable and significantly cheaper alternative to its Opus tier, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro for agentic workloads. The model delivers stronger agentic capabilities at lower price points, directly competing on cost in a market where enterprises evaluate AI tooling ROI.
